enrichment.short_description: Spark Mail is an email client with a Smart Inbox, priority/pin tools, and an AI writing and summarization assistant.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Spark Mail is a focus-first email client with Smart Inbox, Gatekeeper spam screening, and an AI assistant for summarizing and writing emails.

features.title: Smart Inbox
features.description: Automatically organizes incoming email so priority messages surface first.

features.title: Gatekeeper
features.description: Screens and blocks unwanted senders before they reach the inbox.

features.title: AI Assistant
features.description: Finds, summarizes, and takes action on messages in the inbox.

features.title: Spark +AI writing assistant
features.description: Helps compose and rewrite email drafts.

features.title: Send Later & Reminders
features.description: Schedules outgoing messages and sets follow-up reminders.

features.title: Translation
features.description: Translates messages for multilingual email conversations.

features.title: Team collaboration
features.description: Shared inbox and comment tools included from the Plus plan up.

pros: Free plan covers unlimited email accounts with a Smart Inbox and calendar
Native apps for iOS, Android, Mac, and Windows, plus Apple Watch support
Built-in AI assistant and writing tools on paid plans
Supports 9 interface languages
cons: AI meeting notes are capped at 40/month on the Plus plan (unlimited only on Pro)
Some Pro-tier features (workflows, auto-labels/auto-drafts) are still listed as coming soon
Enterprise pricing is quote-only

faqs.question: Does Spark Mail have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes the Smart Inbox, unlimited email accounts, smart notifications, and calendar at no cost.

faqs.question: What does Spark's AI assistant do?
faqs.answer: It can find, summarize, and take action on messages in the inbox, and help draft replies.

faqs.question: What platforms is Spark Mail available on?
faqs.answer: iOS, Android, Mac, Windows, and Apple Watch.

faqs.question: How much does the Plus plan cost?
faqs.answer: €10/month billed monthly, or €8.25/month (€99/year) billed annually.

use_cases.title: Focused personal email
use_cases.description: Individuals use the Free plan's Smart Inbox and Gatekeeper to cut down on inbox noise.

use_cases.title: Team email collaboration
use_cases.description: Teams use shared inboxes, comments, and templates on the Plus/Pro plans to handle email together.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Spark Mail?
paragraphs: Spark Mail is an email client that layers a Smart Inbox, sender screening (Gatekeeper), and an AI assistant on top of existing email accounts.
It's available on iOS, Android, Mac, Windows, and Apple Watch, with team collaboration features like shared comments and templates on paid plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Spark Mail is for
paragraphs: Individuals who want a calmer inbox can use the free tier, while teams that need shared inboxes, AI meeting notes, and CRM integrations can move to the Plus or Pro plans.

enrichment.short_description: Missive is a collaborative email and team inbox app that combines shared inboxes, tasks, chat, and an AI assistant in one interface.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Missive is a shared team inbox that combines email, SMS, chat, and tasks with an AI assistant supporting Anthropic, OpenAI, and Gemini models.

features.title: Shared team inboxes
features.description: Multiple teammates can view, discuss, and assign email in the same inbox.

features.title: Internal comments
features.description: Teams can discuss a message internally before sending a reply.

features.title: Task conversion
features.description: Turns emails into trackable tasks with visibility into team capacity.

features.title: Rules & automations
features.description: Automates repetitive email handling (up to 1,000 rules on the Productive plan).

features.title: AI assistant
features.description: Context-aware writing assistant supporting Anthropic, OpenAI, and Gemini models.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with over 25 apps for added context inside conversations.

features.title: API access
features.description: Available on the Productive plan and above.

pros: 30-day free trial with no credit card required
Native apps for macOS, Windows, iOS, iPadOS, and Android plus a web app
Supports multiple AI providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini) rather than locking into one
SOC 2 Type II compliance on all plans
cons: No free plan — only a time-limited trial
Starter plan caps teams at 5 users
Advanced analytics, SSO, and IP restriction are reserved for the top Business tier

faqs.question: Is there a free version of Missive?
faqs.answer: No, Missive offers a 30-day free trial rather than a permanent free plan.

faqs.question: What platforms does Missive support?
faqs.answer: macOS, Windows, iOS, iPadOS, Android, and a web app.

faqs.question: How much does Missive cost?
faqs.answer: Starter is $14/user/month, Productive is $24/user/month, and Business is $36/user/month, each with a yearly discount option.

faqs.question: Does Missive include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, all plans include an AI assistant with support for multiple AI providers.

use_cases.title: Shared support and sales inboxes
use_cases.description: Teams manage a common email address together with internal comments and task assignment.

use_cases.title: Cross-channel team communication
use_cases.description: Combines email, SMS, and social messages into shared team spaces.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Missive?
paragraphs: Missive is a collaborative inbox built for teams that run their work through email. It lets teammates see the same inbox, comment internally, and turn messages into tasks without changing how they email externally.
It's available as native apps for macOS, Windows, iOS, iPadOS, and Android, alongside a web app, and includes an AI assistant that can use Anthropic, OpenAI, or Gemini models.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Missive is for
paragraphs: Small teams handling shared support, sales, or operations inboxes use Missive to avoid duplicate replies and track who owns what conversation.

enrichment.short_description: Spike turns email into a messenger-style, conversational inbox and adds AI summaries, chat, docs, tasks, and video meetings.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Spike is an AI email client that presents messages as chat conversations and combines email, team chat, docs, tasks, and video calls.

features.title: Conversational email
features.description: Displays email threads as messenger-style conversations while keeping standard email for external recipients.

features.title: AI summaries
features.description: Summarizes unread emails and drafts contextual replies.

features.title: Smart inbox sorting
features.description: Automatically prioritizes incoming messages.

features.title: Built-in video calls
features.description: 1:1 to 10-person video meetings depending on plan.

features.title: Shared inboxes
features.description: Team access to a common inbox on Teamspace plans.

features.title: Collaborative docs and channels
features.description: Docs, groups, and file management alongside email.

pros: Free plan available for both individual email and small teamspaces
Works with existing Gmail, Outlook, and other email accounts rather than requiring a new address
Native apps across iOS, Android, Mac, Windows, and web
Combines email, chat, docs, tasks, and video calls in one app
cons: Free plan limits users to a single email address and 1GB storage
Some collaboration features (video recording, SAML-SSO) are still marked coming soon on Team/Business plans
Higher tiers required for multiple connected email addresses or larger video calls

faqs.question: Is Spike free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Spike offers a Free plan for a single email address as well as a free Starter teamspace for up to 3 members.

faqs.question: Does Spike work with Gmail and Outlook?
faqs.answer: Yes, Spike connects to existing Gmail, Outlook, and other email accounts.

faqs.question: What does Spike's AI do?
faqs.answer: It provides smart inbox sorting, AI summaries of unread email, and contextual draft replies.

faqs.question: What platforms does Spike support?
faqs.answer: iOS, Android, Mac, Windows, and web.

use_cases.title: Conversational personal email
use_cases.description: Individuals use the free plan to read email as chat-style threads with AI summaries.

use_cases.title: Team communication hub
use_cases.description: Small teams use Team/Business Teamspace plans for shared inboxes, docs, and video calls in one place.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Spike?
paragraphs: Spike is an email client that reformats messages as chat-style conversations and adds AI summaries and drafting to existing Gmail, Outlook, and other inboxes.
Beyond email, it bundles team chat, collaborative docs, tasks, and video calls, and is available on iOS, Android, Mac, Windows, and the web.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Spike is for
paragraphs: Individuals who want a chat-like email experience can use the free tier, while small teams use the paid Teamspace plans for shared inboxes and larger video meetings.

enrichment.short_description: Twist is an asynchronous, threaded team messaging app by Doist built to reduce real-time chat interruptions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Twist is Doist's asynchronous team messaging app with threaded, topic-based channels designed to cut down real-time chat interruptions.

features.title: Threaded conversations
features.description: Keeps discussions organized by topic instead of a single scrolling chat feed.

features.title: Structured channels
features.description: Organizes team communication by topic, project, or client.

features.title: Smart notifications
features.description: Reduces notification overload by prioritizing what needs attention.

features.title: Direct messaging
features.description: Private one-on-one conversations alongside team channels.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with Google Drive, GitHub, Zoom, Todoist, Zapier, Asana, and Calendly.

pros: Free plan available with no user cap on internal member accounts
Available on web, iOS, Android, Mac, and Windows
Supports 7 interface languages
Threaded format designed to reduce real-time chat pressure
cons: Free plan limits history to 1 month of comments and messages
Free plan caps integrations at 5
No AI features are mentioned on the site
Enterprise pricing requires contacting sales

faqs.question: Is Twist free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Twist has a free plan limited to 1 month of message history and 5 integrations.

faqs.question: How much is Twist's paid plan?
faqs.answer: The Unlimited plan is €6 per user/month and includes full history, unlimited integrations, and unlimited file storage.

faqs.question: Who makes Twist?
faqs.answer: Twist is made by Doist Inc., the company behind Todoist.

faqs.question: What platforms does Twist run on?
faqs.answer: Web, iOS, Android, Mac, and Windows.

use_cases.title: Distributed team communication
use_cases.description: Remote and async teams use topic-based channels to keep project discussion organized without constant real-time chat.

use_cases.title: Reducing chat interruptions
use_cases.description: Teams that find real-time chat disruptive use Twist's threading and smart notifications to enable deep work.

features: Full history of comments and messages

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Twist?
paragraphs: Twist is a team messaging app from Doist that organizes conversations into threads and topic-based channels instead of a single real-time feed, aiming to reduce interruptions for distributed teams.
It's available on web, iOS, Android, Mac, and Windows, with integrations for Google Drive, GitHub, Zoom, Todoist, Zapier, Asana, and Calendly.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Twist is for
paragraphs: Remote and distributed teams that want organized, async-first discussion instead of constant real-time chat notifications.

enrichment.short_description: Threads is Meta's text-based social networking app, connected to Instagram, for posting updates and public conversations.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Threads is Meta's public conversation app, linked to Instagram, that businesses and creators use to post updates and engage audiences.

features.title: Post creation and sharing
features.description: Publish text, photo, and video posts to a public feed.

features.title: Social engagement
features.description: Likes, comments, reposts, and shares on posts.

features.title: User profiles
features.description: Customizable profiles connected to an Instagram account.

features.title: Search
features.description: Find posts, topics, and accounts across the platform.

features.title: Notifications
features.description: Alerts for replies, likes, and mentions.

pros: Free to use for individuals and businesses
Integrates with existing Instagram accounts and audiences
Backed by Meta's infrastructure and reach
cons: Public, consumer-facing social network rather than a dedicated business communication tool
No pricing tiers, admin/team collaboration features, or business-specific tooling documented on the site
No stated AI, integration, or API capabilities on the official site

faqs.question: Who owns Threads?
faqs.answer: Threads is operated by Meta Platforms as part of the Instagram ecosystem.

faqs.question: Is Threads free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Threads is free to use.

faqs.question: Do I need an Instagram account to use Threads?
faqs.answer: Threads is connected to the Instagram ecosystem and profiles link to Instagram accounts.

use_cases.title: Brand and audience engagement
use_cases.description: Businesses and creators post updates and engage in public conversations to reach audiences.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Threads?
paragraphs: Threads is a text-based social networking app from Meta, connected to Instagram, used for public posts, replies, and conversations similar in format to other short-form social platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Threads is for
paragraphs: Brands, creators, and marketing teams use Threads to publish updates and engage publicly with audiences already reachable through Instagram.

enrichment.short_description: Fibery is a modular work platform that combines relational databases, docs, whiteboards, and automations with a built-in AI assistant.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Fibery combines relational databases, docs, whiteboards, and automations into one connected workspace, with Fibery AI included on every plan.

features.title: Relational databases
features.description: Core structural building block for organizing linked work data.

features.title: Multiple views
features.description: Table, Board, Gallery, List, Timeline, Gantt, Calendar, Report, Map, Feed, Form, and Dashboard views.

features.title: Docs & whiteboards
features.description: Built-in collaborative documentation and visual whiteboard tools.

features.title: Automations & formulas
features.description: Custom workflow automation, including JavaScript automations on Pro plans.

features.title: Fibery AI
features.description: AI features for search, brainstorming, and data analysis across connected work.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Native connectors to 25+ tools including Notion, Asana, Jira, Linear, HubSpot, and Slack.

pros: Free plan for up to 10 users plus 10 guests with AI included
AI included on every paid tier rather than as an add-on
Startup program offers 6 months free of the Pro plan for companies under 42 employees
50% discounts for non-profits, educational, and Ukrainian organizations, plus free access for open-source projects
cons: Enterprise plan requires a minimum of 25 paid users
Advanced permissions and user groups are limited to the Pro tier and above
SSO/SCIM only available on the top Enterprise tier

faqs.question: Does Fibery have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, for up to 10 users and 10 guests, including 10 databases and AI features.

faqs.question: How much does Fibery cost?
faqs.answer: Standard is €12/user/month, Pro is €20/user/month, and Enterprise is €40/user/month, all billed annually with monthly options available.

faqs.question: Is AI included in Fibery's pricing?
faqs.answer: Yes, Fibery AI is included on the Free plan and all paid tiers.

faqs.question: Does Fibery offer startup discounts?
faqs.answer: Yes, companies with fewer than 42 employees can get 6 months of the Pro plan for free.

use_cases.title: Consolidating scattered tools
use_cases.description: Teams replace multiple point tools with Fibery's connected databases, docs, and automations.

use_cases.title: Cross-team project tracking
use_cases.description: Organizations use linked databases and multiple views (Gantt, Kanban, Timeline) to track work across teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Fibery?
paragraphs: Fibery is a modular work platform built around relational databases, offering table, board, timeline, Gantt, and other views alongside docs, whiteboards, and automations.
Fibery AI is included on every plan, from the free tier up, for search, brainstorming, and analysis across connected data.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Fibery is for
paragraphs: Teams that want to replace several disconnected tools with one linked workspace, particularly product and operations teams tracking cross-functional work.

enrichment.short_description: Craft is a notes and docs app with tasks, whiteboards, and AI writing tools, syncing across an individual's devices.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Craft is a notes and docs app combining tasks, calendar, whiteboards, and AI writing assistance with cross-device sync.

features.title: Docs
features.description: Rich document creation and editing.

features.title: Tasks
features.description: Task management embedded directly inside documents.

features.title: Calendar
features.description: Built-in schedule viewing and planning.

features.title: Whiteboards
features.description: Visual collaboration canvases.

features.title: Write with AI
features.description: AI writing assistance with access to Core, Fast, and Max AI models on paid plans.

features.title: Publish & Share
features.description: Direct publishing of documents.

features.title: Templates
features.description: Pre-built document templates.

pros: Free plan includes 15 AI credits and 1GB storage
Recognized as Apple's Mac App of the Year and an Apple Design Awards finalist
Family and Team plans bundle multiple Plus accounts for shared spaces
Integrates with Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, VS Code, and Apple Shortcuts
cons: Free plan is capped at 1,500 blocks of content and only 15 AI credits
No stated Android platform support on the site
AI credits are limited even on the paid Plus plan (50/month)

faqs.question: Is Craft free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes 1,500 blocks of content, 1GB storage, and 15 AI credits.

faqs.question: How much is Craft Plus?
faqs.answer: €4.8/month billed monthly, or €8.0/month billed yearly.

faqs.question: Does Craft integrate with AI tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, it connects with Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, VS Code, and Apple Shortcuts.

faqs.question: Is there a plan for families or teams?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Family plan bundles 2-6 Plus accounts, and the Team plan bundles up to 10.

use_cases.title: Personal notes and planning
use_cases.description: Individuals use Docs, Tasks, and Calendar together for daily notes and planning.

use_cases.title: Shared family or team spaces
use_cases.description: Groups bundle multiple Plus accounts via the Family or Team plan to collaborate in shared spaces.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Craft?
paragraphs: Craft is a notes and document app that embeds tasks, a calendar, and whiteboards alongside writing, with AI assistance available through Core, Fast, and Max models on paid plans.
It has won Apple's Mac App of the Year and was an Apple Design Awards finalist.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Craft is for
paragraphs: Writers, students, and professionals who want notes, tasks, and planning in one document-centric app, with Family and Team plans for shared spaces.

enrichment.short_description: Slite is an AI-powered knowledge base that stays synced with tools like Slack, Linear, and GitHub, and verified by the team.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Slite is an AI knowledge base that syncs with Slack, Linear, and GitHub, flags outdated docs, and lets teams verify content accuracy.

features.title: Slite Agent
features.description: AI search that finds answers across docs and connected tools (Pro plan and above).

features.title: Knowledge drift detection
features.description: Watches Slack, Linear, GitHub, and the codebase to flag when documentation needs updates.

features.title: Doc verification workflow
features.description: Lets teams verify and fact-check content, with suggested fixes on Pro.

features.title: Knowledge Management Panel
features.description: Central view for managing documentation health.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with Slack, Linear, GitHub, Jira, Asana, HubSpot, Intercom, Attio, Google Drive, SharePoint, Salesforce, and BigQuery.

features.title: MCP and API access
features.description: Programmatic and MCP-based access included from the Basic plan.

pros: Free plan available to start
SOC 2 (AICPA) compliance
AI agent can search across many connected third-party tools, not just internal docs
Automated detection of outdated documentation based on tool activity
cons: Slite Agent and search across connected tools require the Pro plan or above
Pro plan AI usage is capped at 50 monthly credits per seat
Enterprise pricing is quote-only
No stated native mobile or desktop app on the fetched pages

faqs.question: Does Slite have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Slite offers a free starting tier alongside its paid Basic, Pro, and Enterprise plans.

faqs.question: What is Slite Agent?
faqs.answer: An AI agent, available on the Pro plan, that searches across Slite docs and connected tools like Slack and GitHub to answer questions.

faqs.question: How much does Slite cost?
faqs.answer: Basic is $10/user/month and Pro is $20/user/month, both billed yearly; Enterprise is custom-priced.

faqs.question: What tools does Slite integrate with?
faqs.answer: Slack, Linear, GitHub, Jira, Asana, HubSpot, Intercom, Attio, Google Drive, SharePoint, Salesforce, and BigQuery.

use_cases.title: Keeping documentation accurate
use_cases.description: Teams use knowledge drift detection to catch docs that go stale as connected tools change.

use_cases.title: Cross-tool knowledge search
use_cases.description: Teams use Slite Agent to search across Slite docs plus Slack, Jira, and other connected apps from one place.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Slite?
paragraphs: Slite is a knowledge base built around keeping documentation accurate: it watches connected tools like Slack, Linear, and GitHub for changes that might make docs outdated, and lets teams verify content.
Its Slite Agent (Pro plan) can search across both Slite docs and other connected tools to answer questions directly.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Slite is for
paragraphs: Teams whose documentation tends to drift out of sync with engineering or operational changes, and who want an AI agent that can answer questions from both docs and connected tools.

enrichment.short_description: Nuclino is a collaborative workspace that unifies docs, knowledge, and lightweight project tracking with an AI assistant called Sidekick.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Nuclino is a fast, unified workspace for docs, knowledge, and projects, with real-time collaboration and an AI assistant called Sidekick.

features.title: Unified workspace
features.description: Combines docs, knowledge base, and project tracking in one place.

features.title: Instant search
features.description: Fast search and hotkeys across the workspace.

features.title: Sidekick AI assistant
features.description: AI features available on the Business plan.

features.title: Canvas
features.description: Visual canvas view for organizing content.

features.title: Real-time collaboration
features.description: Multiple people can edit content simultaneously, included even on the Free plan.

features.title: Apps & integrations
features.description: Connects with external tools; API access from the Starter plan up.

pros: Free plan includes real-time collaboration and two-factor authentication
Used by 12,000+ teams including NASA, Ubisoft, and MIT per the site
API access available starting on the Starter plan
Simple, uncluttered editor noted in user testimonials on the site
cons: Free plan is capped at 50 items, 3 canvases, and 2GB total storage
Sidekick AI is limited to the Business plan
Exact Starter and Business plan prices are not published on the pricing page (quote/checkout only)

faqs.question: Is Nuclino free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan supports up to 50 items, 3 canvases, and 2GB total storage.

faqs.question: Does Nuclino have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, an AI assistant called Sidekick is available on the Business plan.

faqs.question: Who uses Nuclino?
faqs.answer: The site states it's trusted by 12,000+ teams, including NASA, Ubisoft, and MIT.

faqs.question: Does Nuclino offer annual billing discounts?
faqs.answer: Yes, up to 25% off is available with annual billing on the Starter and Business plans.

use_cases.title: Team knowledge base
use_cases.description: Teams centralize documentation and internal knowledge in one searchable workspace.

use_cases.title: Lightweight project tracking
use_cases.description: Small teams track projects alongside docs without a separate project management tool.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Nuclino?
paragraphs: Nuclino is a collaborative workspace that brings docs, knowledge, and lightweight project tracking together, with real-time editing and instant search.
It includes an AI assistant, Sidekick, on its Business plan, and is used by teams including NASA, Ubisoft, and MIT according to the company's site.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Nuclino is for
paragraphs: Teams that want a single, fast workspace for documentation and simple project tracking instead of juggling separate tools.

enrichment.short_description: Tettra is an internal knowledge base with an AI bot, Kai, that answers teammate questions in Tettra or Slack from company docs.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tettra is an internal knowledge base with an AI bot, Kai, that answers employee questions in Slack or Tettra from your documentation.

features.title: Internal knowledge base
features.description: Create docs natively or import from Google Docs, Notion, and PDFs.

features.title: Kai AI bot
features.description: Answers teammate questions in Tettra or Slack by searching knowledge content.

features.title: Slack integration
features.description: Connects the AI bot directly to Slack channels and DMs.

features.title: Verification workflow
features.description: Subject matter experts verify accuracy, identify gaps, and approve edits.

features.title: Usage analytics
features.description: Reporting on content usage included on the Scaling plan.

features.title: API access
features.description: Included on the Scaling plan.

pros: 30-day free trial with no credit card required
AI bot searches both Tettra content and Slack conversations
Rated 4.7 stars on G2 per the site
Verification workflow helps flag outdated content
cons: No permanent free plan — trial only
Scaling plan requires a 10-user minimum
Enterprise pricing is quote-only
No stated native mobile or desktop apps; access is via a web app (app.tettra.co)

faqs.question: Does Tettra have a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 30-day free trial with no credit card required and all features included.

faqs.question: How much does Tettra cost?
faqs.answer: The Scaling plan is $8/user/month with a 10-user minimum, billed monthly or yearly (20% off annually).

faqs.question: What does Tettra's AI bot Kai do?
faqs.answer: It answers teammate questions inside Tettra or Slack by searching the knowledge base content.

faqs.question: Does Tettra integrate with Slack?
faqs.answer: Yes, the AI bot connects directly to Slack channels and DMs.

use_cases.title: Reducing repetitive questions
use_cases.description: Teams use Kai to answer common questions automatically instead of repeating answers in Slack.

use_cases.title: Keeping docs verified
use_cases.description: Subject matter experts use the verification workflow to keep company knowledge accurate.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Tettra?
paragraphs: Tettra is an internal knowledge base that lets teams document processes and answers, then uses an AI bot called Kai to answer teammate questions in Tettra or Slack by searching that content.
It includes a verification workflow so subject matter experts can keep documentation accurate over time.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Tettra is for
paragraphs: Teams that field repetitive internal questions in Slack and want an AI bot to answer from existing documentation instead of a person each time.
